attributes.synopsis:
In the year 2071, humanity has colonized several of the planets and moons of the solar system leaving the now uninhabitable surface of planet Earth behind. The Inter Solar System Police attempts to keep peace in the galaxy, aided in part by outlaw bounty hunters, referred to as "Cowboys". The ragtag team aboard the spaceship Bebop are two such individuals.
Mellow and carefree Spike Spiegel is balanced by his boisterous, pragmatic partner Jet Black as the pair makes a living chasing bounties and collecting rewards. Thrown off course by the addition of new members that they meet in their travels—Ein, a genetically engineered, highly intelligent Welsh Corgi; femme fatale Faye Valentine, an enigmatic trickster with memory loss; and the strange computer whiz kid Edward Wong—the crew embarks on thrilling adventures that unravel each member's dark and mysterious past little by little. 
Well-balanced with high density action and light-hearted comedy, Cowboy Bebop is a space Western classic and an homage to the smooth and improvised music it is named after.

(Source: MAL Rewrite)

sampleJson.description: Trust + receipt layer agents call before and after paying, on Solana mainnet (x402).

trust.returns: live trust intel + signed V6 receipt (score_version intel_renorm_v1)

example_receipt.returns: free no-wallet SAMPLE signed receipt to verify offline before spending

sampleJson.quickstart: Try it free, no wallet: GET /v1/receipts/example -> save twzrd_receipt -> POST /v1/receipts/verify (or npx twzrd-receipt-verifier). Then POST /v1/intel/preflight, pay only if approved via GET /v1/intel/trust/{pubkey}.

info.title: OpenSanctions API
info.description:
## Introduction

The [OpenSanctions](https://www.opensanctions.org) API allows users to search and query
the OpenSanctions [entity graph](https://www.opensanctions.org/docs/entities/). The API
can be used to search people, companies and [other types of entities](https://www.opensanctions.org/reference/#schema).
In particular, it can be used to check the presence of a specific entity on [sanctions
lists](https://www.opensanctions.org/datasets/sanctions/) or in the broader
[OpenSanctions dataset](https://www.opensanctions.org/datasets/).

See also:
* [Example code in Python, JS, and more](https://github.com/opensanctions/api-examples#folders-and-files)
* [OpenSanctions API product page](https://www.opensanctions.org/docs/api/)
* [Account and usage information](https://www.opensanctions.org/service/account/)
* [API frequently asked questions](https://www.opensanctions.org/docs/api/faq/)

### Which endpoint should I use?

The API supports a variety of methods to access and query OpenSanctions data:

* **Entity matching ([/match](#tag/Matching/operation/match_match__dataset__post))**. Use this
  endpoint if you have a set of people or companies you wish to check against
  sanctions or PEPs lists. This API supports fuzzy name matching and using additional
  criteria (e.g. nationality, birth dates, tax identifiers, or addresses) to [score
  entity matches](https://www.opensanctions.org/matcher/) and avoid false positives.

  [Read the tutorial](https://www.opensanctions.org/docs/api/matching/) for an introduction in how to use the matching API. You will also want to familiarise yourself with the [entity data model](https://www.opensanctions.org/docs/entities/) used by the system and the [data dictionary](https://www.opensanctions.org/reference/) entity
  types and properties.

* **Text-based search ([/search](#tag/Matching/operation/search_search__dataset__get))**
  is a useful tool for providing a user-facing search interface, including faceted
  navigation. The search uses [industry standard string syntax](https://www.opensanctions.org/docs/api/search/)
  which can be used to perform field-specific searches, wildcard and fuzzy searches. The
  scores and ranking are generated by the search index and express search relevance, not
  match quality.

* **Entity fetching ([/entities](#tag/Data-access/operation/fetch_entity_entities__entity_id__get))**.
  Retrieve the full details about an entity in the database. The full record will include
  adjacent entities, such as company owners and subsidiaries, family members and associates
  for PEPs, and detailed records for addresses and identification documents linked to
  entities. This endpoint can be used to perform incremental traversal of the OpenSanctions
  graph.

  Read about the [entity data model](https://www.opensanctions.org/docs/entities/) and
  consult the [data dictionary](https://www.opensanctions.org/reference/) to see all entity
  types and their respective properties.

* **Statement-based data access ([/statements](#tag/Data-access/operation/statements_statements_get))**
  allows browsing OpenSanctions data in its most granular form. This can be useful to see
  what source dataset a particular assertion (e.g. the birth date of a person) is sourced
  from. Read about the [statement data model](https://www.opensanctions.org/docs/statements/).

* **OpenRefine Reconciliation API ([/reconcile](#tag/Reconciliation))**. A specialised API
  that lets OpenSanctions data be used by the [OpenRefine](https://openrefine.org/) data
  cleaning tool. Read [the tutorial](https://www.opensanctions.org/articles/2022-01-10-openrefine-reconciliation/)
  to understand how this can be used as an investigative tool.

## Authorization and API keys

To use the OpenSanctions API, you need an API key. Please visit the [API product page](https://www.opensanctions.org/api/) or [contact us](https://www.opensanctions.org/contact/) to learn more. OpenSanctions will issue free API keys to users in academia, non-profit organizations and those working in for-profit journalism.

* [Using API authentication](https://www.opensanctions.org/docs/api/authentication/)

## Dataset scopes

The API provides a sophisticated mechanism for selecting the scope of the data
that sould be queried by a particular request - for example, you can run a name
against all sanctions lists, but not the datasets listing PEPs.

Datasets can be of two types: source datasets - e.g. `us_ofac_sdn` (the US main
sanctions list) contain only entities from one source. These source datasets are
grouped into collections - e.g. [`sanctions`](https://www.opensanctions.org/datasets/sanctions/),
which contains entities from all sanctions lists contained in the database, or
[`peps`](https://www.opensanctions.org/datasets/peps/), which covers all politically
exposed persons sourced from various origins. If you are unsure which dataset you
want to screen against, use [`default`](https://www.opensanctions.org/datasets/default/) (the full
OpenSanctions dataset).

These dataset scopes can be applied to searches and mapping requests by selecting
the desired scope in the URL path of the query: `/search/sanctions` will search
all sanctioned entities, while `/search/us_ofac_sdn` limits the search to only
those entities listed on the US list.

Please note that each indivdual entity (e.g. a sanctioned person) can be linked to
multiple datasets because it has been [de-duplicated](https://www.opensanctions.org/docs/identifiers/).

* See also: [List of datasets in OpenSanctions](https://www.opensanctions.org/datasets/)

parameters.name: dataset

schema.description: Data source or collection name to scope the query to.
schema.title: Dataset

parameters.description: Data source or collection name to scope the query to.

get.description:
Search endpoint for matching entities based on a simple piece of text, e.g.
a name. This can be used to implement a simple, user-facing search. For proper
entity matching, the multi-property matching API should be used instead.

Search queries can include field-specific fitlers, wildcards and fuzzy searches.
See also: [search API documentation](https://www.opensanctions.org/docs/api/search/).

parameters.name: dataset

schema.description: Data source or collection name to scope the query to.
schema.title: Dataset

parameters.description: Data source or collection name to scope the query to.

get.description:
Retrieve a single entity by its ID. The entity will be returned in
full, with data from all datasets and with nested entities (adjacent
passport, sanction and associated entities) included. If the entity ID
has been merged into a different canonical entity, an HTTP redirect will
be triggered.

Intro: [entity data model](https://www.opensanctions.org/docs/entities/).

parameters.name: entity_id

schema.description: ID of the entity to retrieve
schema.title: Entity Id

parameters.description: ID of the entity to retrieve

schema.title: Include adjacent entities (e.g. addresses, family) in response

308.description: The entity was merged into another ID

get.description:
***Beta:** This endpoint is released for wider testing and is not yet recommended
for production use. We welcome feedback. Its interface may change without announcement.

Retrieve entities adjacent to a given entity e.g. passports, sanctions, associates.

This endpoint offers the same information as adjacent entities nested in
[`/entities/{entity_id}`](#tag/Data-access/operation/fetch_entity_entities__entity_id__get),
but offers pagination for cases where the number of results is potentially very large.

parameters.name: entity_id

schema.description: ID of the entity whose graph context was requested
schema.title: Entity Id

parameters.description: ID of the entity whose graph context was requested

308.description: The entity was merged into another ID

get.description:
***Beta:** This endpoint is released for wider testing and is not yet recommended
for production use. We welcome feedback. Its interface may change without announcement.

Retrieve entities adjacent to a given entity for a specific property.

This endpoint offers the same information as adjacent entities nested in
[`/entities/{entity_id}`](#tag/Data-access/operation/fetch_entity_entities__entity_id__get),
but offers pagination for cases where the number of results is potentially very large.

parameters.name: entity_id

schema.description: ID of the entity hose graph context was requested
schema.title: Entity Id

parameters.description: ID of the entity hose graph context was requested

parameters.name: property_name

schema.description: Name of the property to fetch adjacent entities for
schema.title: Property Name

parameters.description: Name of the property to fetch adjacent entities for

308.description: The entity was merged into another ID

get.description:
Reconciliation API, emulates Google Refine API. This endpoint can be used
to bulk match entities against the system using an end-user application like
[OpenRefine](https://openrefine.org). The reconciliation API uses the same
search and matching functions as the matching API and will also produce
scores that reflect additional properties like country or date of birth, if
specified.

Tutorial: [Using OpenRefine to match entities in a spreadsheet](https://www.opensanctions.org/articles/2022-01-10-openrefine-reconciliation/).

parameters.name: dataset

schema.description: Data source or collection name to scope the query to.
schema.title: Dataset

parameters.description: Data source or collection name to scope the query to.

get.description:
No-op basic health check. This is used by cluster management systems like
Kubernetes to verify the service is responsive.

Search index health check. This is used to know if the service has completed
its index building.

ConfigVar.description: A configuration variable for a scoring algorithm.

ConfigVarType.title: ConfigVarType
ConfigVarType.description: The type of a configuration variable.

DataCoverage.title: DataCoverage
DataCoverage.description: Details on the temporal and geographic scope of a dataset.

tags.description: Service metadata endpoints for health checking and getting the application metadata to be used in client applications.
